title: Cut IdentityModel v0.7.0 with acceptedModelFamilies / excludedModelFamilies on harness profiles
description: The AIModelFamily DNA table is currently in freeze-gradient state 1 (pre-shipped — no substrate-internal consumer persists ordinals yet). Cutting IdentityModel v0.7.0 with the consumer fields moves the table to state 2 (substrate-internal with mapping layer). Concrete work: (1) Copy `identity-schemas/v0.6.0/` → `v0.7.0/`. (2) Bump `IdentitySchemaVersion.current = "0.7.0"`. (3) Add `acceptedModelFamilies: [Int]?` and `excludedModelFamilies: [Int]?` fields on harness-profile identities (use `profileType == "harness"` discriminator). (4) Codable invariant: at most one of `acceptedModelFamilies` / `excludedModelFamilies` is non-empty (enforced at decode time). (5) Author migrator `v0.6.0-to-v0.7.0`. (6) Tests in `identity-model-v7-tests.swift`. (7) Update harness identity JSON files (hulk, gemini, codex, openclaw, apple-pi, clide, digikoma, hermes-harness, pi) with their accepted/excluded values per the doctrine (hulk: `[2]` for anthropic-claude; gemini: `[1]` for google-gemini; codex: `[3, 4]` for openai-gpt + openai-codex; apple-pi: `[0]` for apple-foundation; openclaw: `excludedModelFamilies: [0, 1, 2]` for apple/google/anthropic exclusive cluster). (8) Once IdentityModel v0.7.0 ships and a harness identity persists `acceptedModelFamilies: [3]`, the AIModelFamily ordinality table is then in state 2 — any subsequent renumber requires a Migration entry in `AIModelFamilyOrdinalityMappings.migrations[]`.
